design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect five Flickies from each level section by defeating robots, manage Flicky retrieval when scattered, advance through themed zones
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing speed-based progression with precision platforming in isometric perspective; managing unpredictable Flicky AI (green/red variants wander randomly)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rescuing enslaved birds (Flickies) across a mysterious island while collecting Chaos Emeralds and defeating Dr. Robotnik
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Departing from traditional 2D side-scrolling Sonic by adopting isometric perspective with pre-rendered 3D sprites; prioritizes visual style (inspired by Donkey Kong Country) over traditional Sonic physics; mandatory collection mechanic gates progression rather than pure speed

difficulty.lives_system: Starts with four lives; losing a life occurs when hit with no rings or Flickies collected; 1-ups restore lives
difficulty.continues_system: Obtained by collecting ten Sonic-shaped medals throughout levels
difficulty.checkpointing: Zones divided into three acts; progression gates based on Flicky collection (five per section required to advance)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Power-ups available (elemental shields, speed shoes, invincibility) provide mitigation; ring collection serves as health buffer; special stages optional for Chaos Emerald collection

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up monitors (shields, speed shoes, invincibility); Chaos Emeralds unlock final boss and true ending
progression.unlock_mechanism: Collecting ten Sonic medals unlocks continues; obtaining 50 rings + finding hidden Tails or Knuckles unlocks access to special stages; all seven Chaos Emeralds required for Final Fight level
replayability_hooks: Seven Chaos Emeralds to collect across special stages
Multiple Flicky types with different AI behaviors encourage experimentation
True ending locked behind 100% Chaos Emerald completion

world_building_techniques: Themed level zones establish island geography
Doctor Robotnik's robot army presence signals conflict
Flickies as NPCs with distinct personalities (color-coded behavioral differences)
Alternate dimension setting for mysterious tone

boss_design.philosophy: Third act of each zone features solo boss fight against Dr. Robotnik (no Flicky collection); pattern-based combat testing players' ability to dodge and strike in isometric space
notable_bosses: Final Fight boss (unlocked with all seven Chaos Emeralds)

faction_indicators: Doctor Robotnik's enslavement of Flickies establishes clear antagonist
danger_signals: Robot enemies throughout zones indicate active threat
Robotnik's dimension-bridging technology (using Flickies to access rings)
environmental_storytelling: Themed zones (implied island geography across multiple environments)
Power-up monitors as remnants of previous technology/support systems
